sections.heading: Find Your MongoDB Atlas Account
sections.body:
You can manage your Atlas account's settings, unlink your account
from your Google or GitHub account, and configure multi-factor
authentication for your Atlas account.

To find your MongoDB Atlas account:

The Profile Info page opens. From here, you can:

- Access the MongoDB Atlas login page.

- Access Support to view active support cases and
  speak to the Support team.

- Visit the [MongoDB University](https://learn.mongodb.com/)
  to take free courses to become a certified expert on MongoDB.

- Visit the [MongoDB Community Forums](https://community.mongodb.com/)
  to meet other MongoDB developers.

From the left-side navigation bar, you can also:

- Provide your personal information in Profile Info.

  - If you manage your account through your Google Account, visit
    [your Google Account](https://myaccount.google.com/) to manage
    your personal information.

  - If you manage your account through your GitHub Account, visit
    [your GitHub Account](https://github.com/settings/profile) to
    manage your personal information.

- Unlink your MongoDB Atlas account from
  [unlink these accounts](unlink-your-mongodb-account-from-google) or
  [unlink these accounts](unlink-your-mongodb-account-from-github) in
  Profile Info.

- [enable multi-factor authentication](atlas-enable-mfa) in Security.

sections.heading: Change Your Atlas Email Address
sections.body:
You can change the email address that you use to log in to your MongoDB
Atlas account.

> **Note:** If your login information is managed by an identity provider with
> [Configure Federated Authentication](atlas-federated-authentication),
> contact your company's administrator instead.
>
> If you log in with Google SSO, you must
> [unlink these accounts](unlink-your-mongodb-account-from-google)
> before you change your email address.

To change your email address:

- Click Change Email Address under your current email
  address.

- Enter your password. If you have MFA (multi-factor authentication) enabled, authenticate with
  your MFA (multi-factor authentication) method.

- Enter a new email address.

  > **Note:** You can't use an email address associated with another account
  > or with a deleted account.

- Click Save Changes.

  > **Note:** After you save your changes, a banner at the top of the page
  > asks you to verify your email. If you want to cancel your
  > change and continue using your original email address, click
  > Cancel Request on the banner.

A verification email is sent to your new email address once you save
your changes. In that email, click Verify Email to
update your email address.
The project activity feed records your update.

Use your updated email address to log in to Atlas.

sections.heading: Change Your Name in Atlas
sections.body:
You can change the name in your MongoDB Atlas account.

> **Note:** If your personal information is managed by an identity provider with
> [Configure Federated Authentication](atlas-federated-authentication),
> contact your company's administrator instead.

To change your name:

- Under the profile section, update the First Name and/or
  Last Name fields.

- Click Save Changes.

sections.heading: Unlink Your MongoDB Atlas Account from Your GitHub Account
sections.body:
If you [linked](atlas-register-account) your MongoDB Atlas
account to your Github Account, you can unlink it using the following
procedure.

To unlink your MongoDB Atlas account from your Github Account:

- Click Manage your MongoDB Account.

- Click Profile Info.
  The console indicates that:

  - Your Atlas account is linked to your GitHub account.

  - If you intend to unlink your account, you must change your
    password for your Atlas account.

- Click Unlink from GitHub.

- Click Confirm. Atlas sends you an email to your
  email account. This message has instructions to reset your
  Atlas account password.

* Log in to email and locate the Password Reset
  email.

  > **Note:** If you don't reset your password within two hours, submit
  > a new request.

* Click the password reset link in the email to complete
  the password reset process.

* Type your email.

* Type your new Atlas password.

  > **Note:** 
  >
  > Atlas
  >
  >  Password Policy
  >
  > Your Atlas **user account** password must:
  >
  > - Contain at least eight characters.
  >
  > - Exclude your email address and Atlas username.
  >
  > - Differ from your previous twenty four passwords.
  >
  > - Differ from the most commonly used passwords.

* Retype your new password to confirm it.

* Click Save.
  When your password meets the requirements and
  matches in both fields, you have reset your password.
  Atlas displays a success message with a link to
  log in.

- Click Log in to continue.

- Type your email.

- Type your new password.

sections.heading: Unlink Your MongoDB Atlas Account from Your Google Account
sections.body:
If you [linked](atlas-register-account) your MongoDB Atlas
account to your Google Account, you can unlink it using the following
procedure.

To unlink your MongoDB Atlas account from your Google Account:

- Click Manage your MongoDB Account.

- Click Profile Info.
  The console indicates that:

  - Your Atlas account is linked to your Google
    account.

  - If you intend to unlink your account, you
    must change your password for your Atlas account.

- Click Unlink from Google.

- Click Confirm. Atlas sends you an email to your
  Gmail account. This message has instructions to reset your
  Atlas account password.

* Log in to Gmail and locate the Password Reset
  email.

  > **Note:** If you don't reset your password within two hours, submit
  > a new request.

* Click the password reset link in the email to complete
  the password reset process.

* Type your email.

* Type your new Atlas password.

  > **Note:** 
  >
  > Atlas
  >
  >  Password Policy
  >
  > Your Atlas **user account** password must:
  >
  > - Contain at least eight characters.
  >
  > - Exclude your email address and Atlas username.
  >
  > - Differ from your previous twenty four passwords.
  >
  > - Differ from the most commonly used passwords.

* Retype your new password to confirm it.

* Click Save.
  When your password meets the requirements and
  matches in both fields, you have reset your password.
  Atlas displays a success message with a link to
  log in.

- Click Log in to continue.

- Type your email.

- Type your new password.

sections.heading: Delete an Atlas Account
sections.body: This section walks you through deleting your Atlas account.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To delete your Atlas account, you must ensure that you have no:

- Outstanding invoices

- Active clusters

- Projects

- Organizations

> **Important:** If you log in to Atlas through an identity provider, you can't
> delete your account yourself. Contact your identity provider
> to delete your Atlas account.

sections.heading: Delete a Project
sections.body:
To delete a project for an organization:

- You must either have the `Owner` role for the project or have
  the `Organization Owner` role for the project's organization.

- The project must have no outstanding invoices.

- The project must have no active clusters. To learn how
  to view your project's invoices, see [Manage Invoices](atlas-invoices).

sections.heading: Terminate All Clusters
sections.body:
> **Warning:** Terminating a cluster also deletes any backup snapshots for that
> cluster.

To terminate an Atlas cluster:

- Click  next to the cluster you want to disable
  Termination Protection for.

- Click Edit Config from the drop-down menu.

- Click Additional Settings.

- Toggle Termination Protection to Off.

- Click Review Changes.

- Click Apply Changes.

If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy) and
you terminate a cluster, Atlas automatically maintains all
existing snapshots after the termination according to the backup
policy. Atlas retains the [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) for
[restoring a point in time with continuous cloud backup](recover-pit-continuous-cloud-backup) in a static state until
Atlas can no longer use them for continuous cloud backup.

Otherwise, if you are terminating a cluster with Cloud Backup
enabled, you must toggle Keep existing snapshots after
termination to On to retain any existing snapshots
after termination. This option instructs Atlas to keep the
existing snapshots and [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) for the cluster according to
your cluster's
[base snapshot schedule](cloud-provider-retention-policy). If you
have Cloud Backup disabled, this option is unavailable.

- Enter the cluster name.

- Click Terminate.

Atlas terminates the cluster after completing any in-progress deployment changes.

sections.heading: Delete the Project
sections.body:
You can delete a project for an organization either from the
organization's **Projects** view or the project's
**Project Setting** view:

**Projects-view:**

Delete from Projects view

After verifying, click Delete Project again.

**Project-setting-view:**

Delete from Project Setting view

After verifying, click Delete Project again.

sections.heading: Leave an Organization
sections.body:
> **Note:** If you are the only [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) in an
> organization, you must promote
> another member to [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) before you can
> leave an organization.

- Expand the Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

- Click View All Organizations.

For the organization you wish to leave, click its Leave
button to bring up the Leave Organization dialog box.

sections.heading: Delete an Organization
sections.body:
> **Note:** To delete an organization, you must have the
> [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role for the organization.
>
> You cannot delete an organization that has active projects. You must
> delete the organization's projects before you can delete the
> organization.

In the General Settings tab, click Delete.
This displays the Delete Organization dialog box.

Once you delete all active clusters, projects, and
organizations, you are no longer billed for any Atlas activity
and no longer receive Atlas alerts.

sections.heading: Delete your Atlas Account
sections.body:
> **Note:** Once you delete your account there's no way to recover it.
>
> Atlas requires up to two weeks to fully process an account deletion.
> Until then, you can't create a new Atlas account with the email address
> associated with your deleted account.

- Click your name in the top right corner of the Atlas UI.

- Click Manage your MongoDB Account.

- Click Profile Info in the left navigation panel.

* Acknowledge the implications of deleting your
  Atlas account.

* Click Confirm Account Deletion.

> **Note:** If there are any requirements you have not yet met, you are
> prompted to complete them before deleting your account.

You are prompted to verify your identity:

- If you use MFA (multi-factor authentication), you are prompted verify your identity with your
  chosen MFA (multi-factor authentication) authentication method.

- If you use Google SSO, MongoDB
  sends a code to your email address. Use that code to verify your
  identity. Accounts that use Google SSO are automatically
  unlinked as part of the account deletion process.

- If you don't have MFA (multi-factor authentication) configured and don't use Google SSO,
  you are prompted to re-verify your password. Then, MongoDB sends a
  code to your email address. Use that code to finish verifying your
  identity.

Your account is deleted.
